design_philosophy.core_loop: Race against time through themed checkpoint courses across North American landmarks; manage speed, steering, and turbo activation to maintain forward momentum and pass checkpoints before time expires
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure vs. obstacle avoidance; player must balance aggressive speed management against collision risks that cause vehicle flip/crash and time loss
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: High-speed cross-country racing experience with sense of velocity; optional 3D stereoscopic immersion for technologically-forward players
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasis on continuous forward momentum and arcade-style time management rather than realistic racing simulation; generous coasting mechanic (5-10 second grace period when time expires) shows designer belief in player forgiveness over strict punishment; technical showcase of 3D graphics as core feature, not optional enhancement

difficulty.continues_system: Time-based progression gates; failure means stage restart
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoints marked by checkered flags throughout each stage; reaching checkpoint adds additional time or signals stage completion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player can select between two functionally identical car types at start; can change background music during gameplay; hidden level select accessible via button codes suggests designer support for skill-gating circumvention

progression.upgrade_system: Turbo activation system; player can accelerate from 100 km/h base to 255 km/h maximum with turbo engaged
progression.unlock_mechanism: Eight sequential driving stages unlock linearly; stages themed around North American locations (Key West, Big Apple, Gettysburg, Monument Valley, Las Vegas Blvd., Rocky Mountains, Twilight California, Bay Bridge); opponent vehicle quality escalates with each stage
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select code enables non-linear stage access
3D stereoscopic mode toggle adds visual novelty layer
Alternative car selection (Ferrari 328 vs F1 Machine, functionally identical)
Unused third music track 'Still Alive' hidden in ROM suggests developer consideration for extended replay value

world_building_techniques: Stage-specific skylines and environments (Los Angeles, San Francisco, Athens, etc.)
Progressive vehicle sophistication in competitor AI (Volkswagen Beetles to Ferrari Testarossas)
Transamerica race framing as overarching journey structure

danger_signals: Collision mechanics cause vehicle flip/crash states
Obstacle presence throughout courses creates environmental hazards
Escalating competitor vehicle performance signals increasing challenge progression
environmental_storytelling: Themed geographic progression from West Coast to East Coast
Distinct skylines for each stage location create memorable landmarks
Billboard advertisements provide contextual world flavor
Gettysburg stage atmosphere differs from Las Vegas Blvd. creating location-specific identity

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Rad Racer II article with level select, unused assets, developer notes)
wikipedia (Rad Racer article with gameplay mechanics, development, designer/programmer credits, progression structure)
